data.title: Musk to scale up graphics resolution for Grok to do better than AAA games
data.description:
Tesla CEO Elon Musk says his artificial intelligence (AI) company xAI plans to improve the capabilities of its latest model, Grok 3, to scale up graphics resolution for developers to build games better than AAA titles. On Friday, internet personality Mario Nawfal shared a video on social media platform X in which a developer reportedly […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/musk-to-scale-up-graphic-resolution-for-grok/

data.title: Coinbase co-founder: Reached a settlement with the SEC, the lawsuit is expected to be completely dropped
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Coinbase co-founder Brian Armstrong posted on the X platform that Coinbase has reached a settlement with the SEC, and it is expected that the lawsuit will be completely withdrawn, and no fines will be paid, and there will be no changes to its business. This result proves that Coinbase's decision to fight the SEC in court was correct, despite initial doubts. Highlights include: 
· The SEC exceeded its statutory authority and required the delisting of non-securities assets. 
· If compromised, it may harm the US crypto industry and force it to move overseas. 
· This fight is crucial to protecting the rights of customers and the industry, and it is also against government abuse of power. 
Armstrong said that he was proud to challenge the SEC's decision, and they also planned to continue to work with the SEC and advocate for clearer crypto regulations.

data.title: FTX claims from 163 jurisdictions ‘not eligible’ for distributions
data.description:
FTX creditor and advocate Sunil Kavuri shared the list of countries, which includes Russia, China, Egypt, Nigeria and Ukraine.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/ftx-repayments-ineligible-jurisdictions-distributions?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: SEC Staff Agrees in Principle to Dismiss Lawsuit Against Coinbase
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Cointelegraph, SEC staff agreed in principle to dismiss the lawsuit against Coinbase. Perhaps affected by this news, BTC briefly broke through 99,000 USDT. 
Earlier news, the SEC accused 19 tokens of being securities in lawsuits against cryptocurrency exchanges Binance and Coinbase on June 5 and 6, 2023, respectively.

data.title: BinaryX Rebrands to Four: A New Era in DeFi and Meme Fair Launches
data.description: BinaryX has officially rebranded to Four, marking a new chapter in Web3 innovation. This transition reflects the project's commitment to expanding the DeFi ecosystem and fostering a fair, open, and decentralized future.Key Changes in the Rebrand$BNX to $Four Token Swap at a 1:1 Ratio – Holders of $BNX will transition to $Four tokens at an equal conversion rate.New Website and Expanded Utility – The project is launching an updated ecosystem with enhanced functionalities and broader integrations.Introduction of Four.meme – A new meme fair launch platform, incubated by BinaryX, designed to enable community-driven token launches.

data.title: Iowa Proposes Bill to Allow State Treasury Investments in Bitcoin
data.description: According to Foresight News, Bitcoin Magazine reports that a new bill has been introduced in Iowa, proposing to permit the state treasury to invest in Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies. This legislative move aims to explore the potential benefits of digital assets for state financial management.

data.title: Michael Saylor Suggests U.S. Should Acquire Significant Bitcoin Holdings
data.description: According to PANews, Michael Saylor, the founder of Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy), has expressed his views on national cryptocurrency investments. Saylor stated that only a nation-state has the capability to purchase 20% of all Bitcoin, and he believes that the United States should be the one to make such an acquisition.

data.title: Bybit lifts curtain on liquidation data following underestimated figures
data.description:
Bybit has made liquidation data public via API, improving transparency for traders after CEO Ben Zhou challenged underreported crypto liquidation figures.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bybit-lifts-curtain-on-liquidations-data-following-underestimated-figures?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Binance Wallet Launches Exclusive Staking Rewards: Earn Up to 50% APR with Astherus and Kernel for 16 Days!
data.description: Binance Wallet has partnered with Astherus and Kernel DAO to offer an exclusive staking campaign, where users can earn up to 50% APR for 16 days on Yield+ BNB Astherus Kernel Strategy.Promotion Period:February 20, 2025 (00:00 UTC) - March 8, 2025 (23:59 UTC)Stake and Earn with Boosted APRDuring this limited-time promotion, eligible users who stake at least 0.5 BNB via Binance Wallet into the Yield+ BNB Astherus Kernel Strategy will receive:- 19% Boosted APR – On top of the real-time APR.- Rewards Distributed in Astherus Future Tokens – A total reward pool of $250,000 (first-come, first-served).- Daily Percentage Rate (DPR) of 0.13% – The total APR can reach up to 50%, but the base APR may fluctuate.How It Works:Successfully stake your BNB in the Yield+ Astherus BNB Strategy via Binance Wallet.Once staked, you will receive asBNB tokens on the BNB Smart Chain.The Kernel DAO does not provide a liquid staking token after staking, so users must check their staking position on Kernel DAO’s website.Important Campaign Details- Maximum Staking Limit: 3,500 BNB per user- Maximum Staking Duration: 16 days for boosted rewards- TVL Cap for Prize Pool: $30,000,000 in BNBTotal Rewards Pool: $250,000 in Astherus Future TokensNote:Once the $30M TVL cap is reached, new users will no longer receive the 19% Boosted APR but can still earn the base APR. Ensure you check the prize pool allocation before staking.
